title: Unified deep learning framework for many-body quantum chemistry via Green's functions
core_conclusion: MBGF-Net is a graph neural network that learns the many-body Green's function self-energy from DFT features, unifying predictions of ground-state and excited-state electronic properties (ionization potentials, optical gaps, density matrices) in a single model at GW or coupled-cluster accuracy. Trained on as few as 2,000 molecules, it surpasses models trained on 100,000 molecules in data efficiency and generalizes to systems up to four times larger than any training example, demonstrating scale transferability driven by physically meaningful, locally composable correlation patterns.
why_it_matters: First unified ML model for both ground and excited states via a single Green function prediction; 50-fold data efficiency improvement over prior state-of-the-art; demonstrated 4x size transferability for Si nanoclusters; built-in physical interpretability via QP renormalization and natural orbital occupancies; enables data-driven DMFT impurity solvers for correlated materials.
why_nature: Principled physics-motivated unification rather than an engineering hack; cross-domain scope (molecules, nanomaterials, strongly correlated systems); fully open code and data; striking quotable efficiency benchmark (2000 vs 100000 training samples).
added_value_to_my_research: SAIAO equivariance construction -> analytically invariant spatial-feature encoding for urban GNNs without learning symmetry from data
Imaginary-frequency grid template -> non-uniform temporal-frequency basis for spatiotemporal activity models (ambulance demand, mobility flows)
Ensemble active refinement for chemical imbalance -> uncertainty-driven spatial data collection strategy for data-scarce geographic regions
Unified self-energy latent target -> single latent spatial health potential field from which multiple outcomes (utilization, prevalence) are postprocessed
System-size transferability benchmark -> physics-informed local interaction constraints enabling neighborhood-to-city scale transfer in geospatial foundation models

figures.caption: MBGF-Net workflow and GNN architecture: from DFT input to orbital graph, message-passing layers, and postprocessed many-body properties
MBGF-Net predictions for QM7/QM9: training curves, scatter plots, and case studies including extrapolation to NHS-biotin
Si nanocluster excited-state predictions: band and optical gaps vs. cluster size, defect states, and photoemission/optical spectra
Azobenzene band-gap predictions as a function of CN=NC dihedral angle: interpolation and out-of-distribution extrapolation
Strong-correlation regime: QP renormalization weights, natural orbital occupancies for C-O bond breaking, and FNO-CCSD potential energy curves
